2604.28075.title: Repetition over Diversity: High-Signal Data Filtering for Sample-Efficient German Language Modeling
2604.28075.paper_summary: Recent research has shown that filtering massive English web corpora into high-quality subsets significantly improves training efficiency. However, for high-resource non-English languages like German, French, or Japanese, aggressive filtering creates a strategic dilemma: should practitioners prioritize diversity by training once on large amounts of lightly filtered web data, or prioritize quality by strictly filtering for a high-quality core and repeating it over multiple epochs? We investigate this trade-off for German by constructing hierarchical quality filters applied to 500M web documents, comparing multi-epoch training on the filtered subsets against single-pass training on a diverse corpus. Our experiments across multiple model scales and token budgets show that repeating high-quality data consistently outperforms single-pass training on larger, less filtered sets. Notably, the performance gap persists even after 7 epochs. Our findings suggest that for non-English LLMs, semantic concentration through quality filtering offers a more viable path to efficient language modeling than simply maximizing unique data volume. We release our German language models (called Boldt), as well as our cleaned evaluation benchmarks to the research community. Our experiments indicate that they achieve state-of-the-art results despite training on 10-360x fewer tokens than comparable models.
2604.28075.paper_summary_zh: 最近的研究顯示，將大量的英語網絡語料庫過濾成高質量的子集顯著提高了訓練效率。<lb/>然而，對於德語、法語或日語等高資源非英語語言來說，激進的過濾會造成一個戰略困境：實踐者應該優先考慮多樣性，通過在大量輕度過濾的網絡數據上進行一次訓練，還是應該優先考慮質量，通過嚴格過濾出高質量核心並在多個時期重複訓練？<lb/>我們通過對500M網頁文檔構建分層質量過濾器來研究這一權衡，將在過濾子集上的多次訓練與在多樣化語料庫上的單次訓練進行比較。<lb/>我們在多個模型規模和標記預算下的實驗顯示，重複使用高質量數據始終優於在較大、過濾較少的數據集上的單次訓練。<lb/>值得注意的是，性能差距在經過7個時期後仍然存在。<lb/>我們的研究結果表明，對於非英語的LLM，通過質量過濾實現語義集中提供了一條比單純最大化唯一數據量更可行的高效語言建模路徑。<lb/>我們將我們的德語語言模型（稱為Boldt）以及我們清理過的評估基準釋放給研究社群。<lb/>我們的實驗表明，儘管在比可比較模型少10-360倍的標記上進行訓練，但它們仍然達到了最先進的結果。
2604.28075.author: Ansar Aynetdinov et.al.
2604.28075.authors: Ansar Aynetdinov, Patrick Haller, Alan Akbik
